23.reason: The loop condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of items read (1) or EOF, not -1. The input ends with -1 as a value, not as a signal to stop reading. This loop will never terminate properly.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The loop condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of items read (1) or EOF, not -1. The input ends with -1 as a value, not as a signal to stop reading. This loop will never terminate properly.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition should check for -1 as the input value, not the return value of scanf. scanf returns the number of items read, not the input value. This loop will never terminate as intended.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [incorrect loop condition: scanf returns number of items read, not the value; should compare n != -1]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The loop condition checks if the return value of scanf is -1, but scanf returns the number of items successfully read. It should check if the read value 'n' is -1 to break the loop. Also, this logic consumes the -1 terminator incorrectly or fails to stop at the right time depending on input buffering, but the primary issue is the condition logic for stopping.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition (scanf("%d",&n))!=(-1) is incorrect. scanf returns the number of items successfully read, or EOF. It does not return the value read into n. This causes the loop to terminate incorrectly or behave unpredictably. It should check if n == -1 after reading.

31.reason: [Incorrect array passing: 's[100]' accesses out-of-bounds element instead of passing the array base address. Should be 's'.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrect argument. Should be `pop(s)` to pass the array, not `s[100]` which is an out-of-bounds element.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrect argument: should be 's' (the array name), not 's[100]'. 's[100]' is out of bounds and not a valid array reference.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  s[100] is out-of-bounds; should be pop(s)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [s[100] is out‑of‑bounds array element; should be pop(s)]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Passing s[100] passes a single integer (out of bounds access) instead of the array pointer. It should be pop(s).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Passing s[100] passes a single integer (out of bounds access) instead of the array. The function expects an array (pointer), so it should be pop(s). Also, the function definition int pop(int s[100]) is misleading but technically accepts a pointer, however calling with s[100] is invalid.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Passing s[100] (an out-of-bounds element) instead of the array s. Should pass 's' to match function parameter expecting an array.]
